pain_points: Tried diets before but regained weight; frustrated by conflicting calorie advice online; doesn't trust generic 1200-calorie recommendations
motivation: Understand personal calorie needs accurately so this attempt actually sticks
trigger_moment: Steps on scale after holiday season or sees an old photo and decides today is the day to restart

persona: Fitness Newbie Planning First Cut or Bulk
pain_points: Overwhelmed by gym culture jargon; doesn't know where macros or calories should start; fears eating too little or too much and stalling progress
motivation: Get a concrete starting number to follow so training effort isn't wasted by poor nutrition
trigger_moment: Signs up for a gym membership or buys a program and realizes nutrition guidance is missing

pain_points: Period apps give inaccurate predictions causing anxiety; confused by cycle variation; worried about conceiving or avoiding pregnancy with unreliable data
motivation: Understand fertile windows and cycle patterns to feel prepared and in control
trigger_moment: Decides to try to conceive or has an unplanned late period that triggers anxiety about dates

pain_points: Uncertain how many calories she actually needs during pregnancy or breastfeeding; afraid of generic advice that ignores her specific situation; confused by contradictory sources
motivation: Confirm healthy weight gain targets or postpartum recovery nutrition without guessing
trigger_moment: Doctor visit leaves her without specific numbers, so she searches online to fill the gap herself

pain_points: Standard calculators assume sedentary baselines and underestimate needs; frustrated that results feel generic and ignore training load; wants sport-specific macro splits
motivation: Dial in fueling precisely enough to support training, recovery, and body composition goals simultaneously
trigger_moment: Hits a performance plateau or sees body composition stall despite consistent training and clean eating

pain_points: Worries about silent risk factors like high body fat or low water intake; distrusts single-metric tools like BMI alone; wants reassurance backed by numbers
motivation: Get a credible snapshot of multiple health markers at once to either act on or stop worrying about
trigger_moment: Reads a health article, attends a company wellness event, or gets routine bloodwork that surfaces an unexpected number
